title: delegation — field classification
description: role_delegations metadata is largely internal-tier (delegator + delegate identity is already visible to both parties by the domain rules). impersonation_sessions is heavier — the operator identity + reason + support ticket reference are internal-tier (visible only to compliance-authorized readers), and the session_token_hash is confidential (equivalent to a password hash for the short-lived impersonation credential).

restricted: ImpersonationSession.session_token_hash (equivalent to a short-lived password hash — never surfaced on the wire; access limited to the enforce_active_session middleware + the token-scrub job)
regulated_health: ImpersonationSession.reason WHEN session.metadata.compliance_regime='hipaa' — may reference minor's medical care (Wave 2 hook, not currently active)
audit_log: RoleDelegation.* (subject to HasActivityLog + HasAudit trail rules)
ImpersonationSession.* (subject to HasAudit heavy-trail rules — every field logged on every write; correlation IDs preserved for the whole session lifetime)

notes: RoleDelegation.reason + revoke_reason are marked confidential because they may reference internal HR context ('Alice is on medical leave until 2026-08-15'). Public-tier readers of the /me/delegations surface see the reason (delegator + delegate know it), but cross-user reads via the admin surface are permission-gated.

ImpersonationSession.reason + support_ticket_reference are confidential because they may reference customer-support context that is itself sensitive (a customer's billing dispute, a suspected security issue on the target's account). Access is scoped to the impersonator + security + super_admin + dpo via ImpersonationSessionPolicy.

The impersonator_display_name snapshot is confidential because it identifies a specific Stackra staff member's real name — internal-tier by our classification but revealing enough that we treat it as confidential for consistency with employment records.

session_token_hash is RESTRICTED — even the impersonator does not see the hash after session start (they only ever knew the raw token, which is not persisted). It exists solely for the middleware's lookup + is scrubbed at 30 days.
GDPR Art. 17 erasure: on target User erasure, the ImpersonationSession row cascades but the parallel HasAudit stream is retained under Art. 17(3)(e) legal claims defence.
